name: Pino
what_it_does: Extremely fast Node.js JSON logger. Pino is 5x faster than Winston by using JSON.stringify and asynchronous log transport (pino-transport). Outputs newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON) for easy log aggregation. Production-oriented — designed for high-throughput servers where logging overhead matters. pino-pretty for human-readable development output. Used by Fastify's built-in logger.
use_cases: Log structured JSON from Node.js servers and agent backends with minimal performance overhead
Build structured logging for agent systems where log aggregation (Datadog, Loki, Splunk) requires JSON format
Implement child loggers with request context (request ID, user ID) that automatically attach to all log entries in a request
Stream high-volume agent logs to multiple destinations using pino's transport API without blocking the event loop
Use as Fastify's recommended logger for consistent structured logging across the entire HTTP server
not_for: Browser logging — Pino is Node.js only; use browser-appropriate loggers for frontend
Human-readable development logs without pino-pretty — raw Pino output is JSON; add pino-pretty for readable output
Complex log transformations in-process — Pino offloads transformations to transports to keep main thread fast
best_when: You're building a production Node.js server or agent backend where logging performance matters and you need structured JSON logs for aggregation.
avoid_when: You need a fully-featured logging framework with transport plugins (email alerts, Slack) — Winston has a richer transport ecosystem. Pino is optimized for performance, not flexibility.

auth.notes: No authentication — local logging library. Transport destinations may require auth configured separately.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local logging. Async transport prevents blocking main thread.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Pino itself rarely errors — transport errors surface via error events. JSON output is machine-readable. pino-pretty makes development logs human-readable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Logging is a side effect — idempotency not applicable.
known_agent_gotchas: Pino's async transport (worker_threads) means logs may not flush on process crash — use pino.destination({sync: false}) only for graceful shutdowns; sync mode for critical crash logs
Log level filtering is checked before serialization — set level to 'info' to skip debug serialization overhead entirely; avoids cost of building objects that get dropped
Child loggers share parent serializers and transports — child logger context (requestId) merges with parent but serializers apply to both parent and child
pino-pretty should NOT be used in production — it's slow; pipe raw Pino output to pino-pretty only during development
Circular references in logged objects cause JSON.stringify to throw — Pino has safe-stable-stringify option for circular reference handling
pino v9 changed transport worker thread model — existing pino-transport compatible packages may need updates; check version compatibility

security_score_components.security_notes: Log output may contain sensitive data — implement redaction for passwords, tokens, PII using Pino's redact option. Fast serialization means sensitive objects log quickly if not redacted.
